I ran `tenex-edge launch <agent> --channel new_channel` and NOTHING was created on the relay -- nothing at all... why is a new channel not immediately provisioned??

Found that the `--channel` flag passes the string as-is to an existing group h-value without any creation logic; suggested using the interactive picker or a two-step create-then-launch approach as workarounds.

Advised restarting the daemon so it reconnects with the correct port.
Fixed. Now restart the daemon so it picks up the corrected relay URL.
how can I run with a different tenex-edge config? some env variable to point to a different tenex-edge config that the one in ~/.tenex-edge?
Pointed out that `TENEX_EDGE_HOME=/path/to/your/dir` can be used to override the default config directory.
and something launched via `tenex-edge launch` will respect that env variable even when ran through the agent harness hooks?
Confirmed that `TENEX_EDGE_HOME` is explicitly forwarded into spawned tmux sessions, so the agent and all harness hooks see the same value.

Implemented printing home and relay configuration on every invocation.
